sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The white diamond and purple square seem to have moved closer together. Let me check the current state to see where things are now and if the magenta pixel has reached the dark gray diamond.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Key objects:")
for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c in ['W', 'M', 'G'] or (c == 'p' and n['pixels'] >= 20):
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  color={c} px={n['pixels']} center=({center_r},{center_c}) boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if magenta is now at the dark gray diamond position
# If the purple square is now at the dark gray diamond, clicking it might finish the level
# Let me also check if there's a new purple square somewhere
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
